seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with Claude Tool Use + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with Claude Tool Use + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: Claude Tool Use ai coding assistant

content.intro: Building an AI Coding Assistant with Claude Tool Use enables powerful code-aware agents that can read repositories, execute code in sandboxes, and iteratively debug issues. However, tool use chains in coding workflows often cascade into errors from early tool failures or produce insecure code with vulnerabilities. Vex guardrails ensure reliable, secure outputs by verifying tool responses against code correctness, security, and compliance standards.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with Claude Tool Use
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture centers on Claude models with native tool use for agentic coding workflows. User requests trigger Claude to call tools like `read_file`, `write_file`, `execute_code`, and `run_tests` within a sandboxed environment. Repository context is injected via tools, enabling the agent to analyze codebases, propose fixes, validate with tests, and iterate until passing.

Tool calls form chains: reading files → analyzing → editing → testing → debugging. Parallel tools may fetch dependencies or scan vulnerabilities. Extended reasoning ensures consistency between thought process and code output, with screen-based computer use avoided through API-driven sandbox execution.

Vex wraps Claude's `messages.create()` tool_use responses to verify code outputs before execution, catching cascading failures and ensuring security compliance.

risks.name: Cascading tool failures
risks.description: Early file read or dependency fetch failures propagate through edit/execute chain, producing invalid code
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies each tool response before chain continuation

risks.name: Insecure code generation
risks.description: Agent generates code with SQL injection, hardcoded credentials, or unsafe deserialization
risks.mitigation: Vex scans for security vulnerabilities and credential leakage

risks.name: Test validation bypass
risks.description: Agent claims fixes without running tests or ignores failing test results
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces test execution and validates passing results

risks.name: License/dependency violations
risks.description: Introduces incompatible licenses or malicious dependencies
risks.mitigation: Vex checks dependency safety and license compliance

vexIntegration.title: Python Vex Verification Wrapper
vexIntegration.description: Wrap Claude tool responses with Vex for code safety verification
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import verify

# After Claude messages.create()
response = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
    tools=tools,
    messages=messages
)

# Extract tool output (generated code)
code_output = extract_code_from_tool(response)

# Vex verification
verified = verify(
    code=code_output,
    checks=[
        "code_correctness",
        "security_vulnerabilities",
        "no_credential_leakage",
        "dependency_safety",
        "license_compliance"
    ],
    risk_profile="medium"
)

if verified:
    # Safe to execute
    execute_in_sandbox(code_output)
else:
    # Request Claude to fix issues
    pass
